name: Water nitrogen sensor (NtcA + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: A self-powered sensor for nitrogen status in water bodies (relevant to eutrophication and algal blooms). The native cyanobacterial NtcA nitrogen regulator gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the photosynthetic chassis Synechocystis — which runs on light and CO2.

input.detects: Cellular nitrogen status, reporting nitrogen availability in water (eutrophication-relevant)
input.operatingRange: NtcA responds to the C/N balance via 2-oxoglutarate

sensing.sensorMechanism: NtcA, the global cyanobacterial nitrogen regulator, is activated by 2-oxoglutarate (high under nitrogen limitation) and turns on NtcA-dependent promoters.
sensing.signalFlow: Nitrogen limitation -> NtcA activates an NtcA promoter -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence reports nitrogen status. Self-powered by photosynthesis.

parts.name: NtcA-dependent promoter (e.g. PglnA)
parts.notes: Activated by 2-OG-bound NtcA under N limitation.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak promoter

parts.name: dCas9-ω activator
parts.source: Yao et al. 2016 / CRISPRi-a in Synechocystis

output.result: Fluorescence reflects nitrogen status of the water.

performance.limitOfDetection: NtcA reports the cellular C/N balance (module-validated).
performance.notes: NtcA nitrogen sensing and Synechocystis CRISPRi-a are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Photoautotrophic chassis enables long-term, self-powered deployment.

safety.biocontainment: Light/CO2-dependent growth; add inducible-essential containment for outdoor use.
safety.notes: Self-powered (photosynthesis) — suited to long-term water-body monitoring.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak promoter; check Synechocystis off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: NtcA promoter -> sgRNA (native NtcA). TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. Integrate at neutral sites; CyanoGate.

buildSteps.title: Transform Synechocystis 6803
buildSteps.description: Use natural transformation; select; segregate fully.

buildSteps.title: Deploy / expose
buildSteps.description: Expose to water + a nitrogen series under light.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native Synechocystis NtcA nitrogen sensor with cyanobacterial CRISPRa
partsSources: Herrero et al. / NtcA nitrogen regulation (cyanobacteria)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
